qt: In employment stability, we have the following options except __________.
op: Hold employment constant by maintaining the trained workforce
Hold employment constant and labor is treated as a fixed cost

qt: Which of the following inventory management strategies helps to reduce inventory levels and minimize inventory costs?
As per the Hawthorn studies, which of the following statements best describes about worker productivity?
op: It increases when worker receive attention and feel valued.
It is solely determined by physical working conditions.
It is unaffected by social factors.
It is higher in isolated work environments.

qt: In which of the following method, the time standard for a job can be set in a laboratory away from the actual production operation.
While using time studies methods in labor standards and work measurement, we use ____________ and ______________ to calculate the standard time of a job.
op: Total Normal time and Allowance factor

qt: Which one of the following is not a popular sequencing rule?
Which of the following is the alternate name of non-instantaneous resupply?
In ABC analysis, which category of items typically represents the low annual dollar volume?
In which of the following models, the beginning inventory is equal to order quantity?
Which of the following refers to the reorder point (ROP)?
op: The point at which inventory must be reordered to avoid stockouts

qt: Which of the following is true regarding labor standards and work measurement?
op: MTM table is used in calculating job time with historical experience.
MTM table is used in calculating the time of micro moments in a job element.
Allowance factor is used in predetermined time standards
In time studies, we use MTM table and allowance factor

qt: Which one of the following options involves the establishment of employees’ roles and responsibilities?
Which one of the following statements is the primary objective of job design?
op: To increase the number of employees in the organization.
To create jobs that enhance employee satisfaction and performance.
To reduce the amount of training required for new hires.

qt: Which of the following is not included in capacity options in the context of aggregate planning strategies?
op: Develop a product mix of counter-seasonal items.
The trade-off between capacity, inventory, backlog/lost sales.
Increase in inventory during low-demand periods to meet high demand in future.
Varying workforce size by hiring or layoff as per the need.

qt: Which of the following is not included in the demand options in the context of aggregate planning strategies?
op: Develop a product mix of counter-seasonal items
Influencing demand through advertising and promotion campaigns
Increase in inventory during low-demand periods to meet high demand in future

qt: What happens to a scheduled receipt when the goods are received into inventory?
op: It is transferred to the vendor’s records.
It is converted into a new planned order.
It becomes part of the on-hand inventory and is closed out.
It remains open until the end of the production cycle.

qt: Which of the following is not true regarding aggregate planning?
op: Aggregate planning is the balance between demand and supply.
If the aggregate plan is followed entirely, it will help meet the firm's strategic objectives.
Aggregate planning and forecast are the same thing.
Aggregate planning is the planned production for which manufacturing management is to be held responsible.

qt: Which of the following costs is most critical in aggregate planning in the service sector?
In which aggregate planning strategy, the firm is dependent on the external source?
op: Use of inventory as a lever
Use of part-time workers to meet the demand
Use of hiring and firing of workers to meet the demand

qt: Which of the following parameters constitute the yield management matrix?
Allocating resources to customers at prices that will maximize the revenue is referred to as _______.

For an aggregated plan, using level strategy, if we maintain the constant workforce against the lowest demand of the planning period, then which of the following can be used as a lever to meet further demand?
Who is responsible for releasing planned orders in the MRP system?
Which of the following is true about scheduled receipts?
op: They are future orders that are automatically released by the MRP system.
They are not yet committed and are still in planning stages.
They represent orders that have been completed and added to on-hand inventory.
They are orders that have been placed and show quantities and expected completion dates.

qt: Which aggregate planning strategy uses inventory as a lever?
Which method is used when we need an optimum solution to an aggregate planning problem and when we need to allocate operating capacity to meet forecasted demand?
Which risks are associated with the chase strategy in aggregate planning?
op: This may lead to insecure and unhappy employees
Risk associated with over or underestimation of market demand

qt: Considering the level strategy in aggregate planning strategies, which of the following is used as a lever?
What do scheduled receipts represent in the MRP system?
op: Orders that are still under review and have not been confirmed
Planned orders that have not yet been released
Orders placed with manufacturing or vendors that are committed to making or buying

qt: Which of the following is true regarding transportation methods?
op: It is not a trial-and-error method
Transportation method can be used when the problem is viewed as one of allocating operating capacity to meet forecast demand
It produces an optimum solution to an aggregate planning problem
All the above are true regarding transportation method

qt: Labor standards can be developed in all the following ways except ___________.
All the following are the functions of human resource management, EXCEPT:
In ABC analysis, which category of items typically represents the highest value but the smallest percentage of total items?
Which of the following industries are most likely to benefit from the application of Johnson's rule?
Which of the following is not a characteristic of a level strategy in aggregate planning?
op: Use inventory or idle time as a buffer.
The level strategy involves keeping the workforce constant.
In the level strategy, daily production varies with the demand.

qt: Annual ordering cost is calculated by which of the following formulas?
Which one of the following cannot be considered as benefit due to JIT implementation?
Which of the following is not a building block of just-in-time production?
Just in Time environment is based on which of the following options?None of the given options
Which of the following is not a supporting goal of JIT system?
When should a planned order be released according to the MRP objectives?
op: Immediately after the order is planned
As soon as the order is placed
After receiving confirmation from the vendor
When the planned order release date arrives

qt: Which one of the following is not included in “housekeeping” for reducing waste?
In aggregate planning, when demand will be met by increasing or decreasing capacity through hiring and layoff of workers, without maintaining any inventory, we are using ____________ strategy.
